name: LiteDB
description: LiteDB is a lightweight, embedded NoSQL document database for .NET, inspired by MongoDB. It stores data as BSON (Binary JSON) documents in a single data file with no server or configuration required. LiteDB provides a fluent API, LINQ query support, ACID transactions, and cross-collection references, making it ideal for desktop, mobile (Xamarin), and small web applications that need a zero-configuration embedded database.
core_strengths: Zero-configuration embedded database — single DLL under 450 KB
MongoDB-like document API with BSON storage and POCO mapping
LINQ query support with expression-based filtering and indexing
Cross-collection document references (DbRef) for relational patterns
Built-in FileStorage for storing files and streams (like GridFS)
Cross-platform: .NET Framework, .NET Core, Xamarin, Unity, UWP
best_use_cases: Desktop and mobile application local storage
Single-file application data format for .NET apps
Per-user or per-account local data stores in web applications
Embedded document storage where MongoDB client-server is overkill
Prototyping and small-scale .NET applications with zero setup
remarks: Documents are limited to 1 MB each. WAL mode is available for crash recovery. Indexes are stored per collection. Not designed for high-concurrency multi-process access. The API uses a reader-writer lock pattern — readers don't block each other, but writes are serialized per collection.
